name: Common Threads
summary: Common Threads is a First Nations-led Australian organisation that runs advocacy and campaign work on truth-telling, Treaty, land rights, and climate justice.
notes: Official organisation website clearly identifies mission and leadership.

name: SOSBlak Australia SA
summary: SOS Blak Australia is a grassroots Aboriginal rights campaign best known for mobilising against forced closure of remote Aboriginal communities.
notes: Domain is cited by SBS/NITV and Cultural Survival as the campaign site, but current activity and ownership are unclear.

name: No Underground Coal Gasification at Leigh Creek
summary: This campaign supports Adnyamathanha community opposition to underground coal gasification near Leigh Creek and Copley.
notes: Best match is a dedicated campaign page hosted by Original Power rather than a standalone domain.

name: Pormpuraaw Aboriginal Shire Council
summary: Pormpuraaw Aboriginal Shire Council is the local government authority delivering civic services and community development in Pormpuraaw.
notes: Official council website.

name: Lake Tyers Aboriginal Trust
summary: Lake Tyers Aboriginal Trust is an Aboriginal-governed body that manages trust land, governance, and community matters at Lake Tyers.
notes: Official trust website with governance and contact information.

name: University of Adelaide
summary: The University of Adelaide is a major South Australian university institution focused on higher education and research.
notes: The adelaide.edu.au domain is official and now reflects the post-2026 Adelaide University transition while retaining University of Adelaide context.

name: Mandingalby Yidinji Aboriginal Corporation
summary: Mandingalbay Yidinji Aboriginal Corporation is the native title representative body for Mandingalbay Yidinji people and their country near Cairns.
notes: Name appears with spelling variants (Mandingalby/Mandingalbay); this page is referenced via native title listings and provides direct MYAC details.

name: Nanum Wungthim Land & Sea Management Group
summary: Nanum Wungthim is an Indigenous ranger group in Napranum that delivers coastal, cultural heritage, and land-and-sea management work.
notes: No standalone organisation-run website found; used the National Indigenous Australians Agency profile page.

name: Lachlan Kalari River Aboriginal Water Council
summary: This entity appears to be an Aboriginal water governance and advocacy body associated with the Lachlan (Kalari) river region.
notes: No official organisation website located; this is a registry-style listing and explicitly states it is not the official website.

name: Government Architect NSW
summary: Government Architect NSW provides strategic design leadership to improve architecture, urban design, and landscape outcomes across NSW.
notes: Official NSW Government Architect website.

name: Lachlan Kalari River Aboriginal Water Committee
summary: This committee appears to be part of Aboriginal advisory structures informing water planning and management in the Lachlan region.
notes: Exact committee name is referenced in water-sector materials, but no dedicated committee-run site was found; this NSW Water page is the closest official program context.

name: Full Stop Australia
summary: Full Stop Australia is a national specialist service providing trauma-informed support and 24/7 counselling related to sexual, domestic, and family violence.
notes: Official organisation website.

name: Change the Record
summary: Change the Record is a First Nations-led coalition campaigning to end over-incarceration and systemic violence against Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander peoples.
notes: Official coalition website.

name: Wenlock Catchment Management Group Inc
summary: Wenlock Catchment Management Group Inc is a Cape York catchment group focused on river and landscape stewardship in the Wenlock region.
notes: No standalone website found; group is listed through Queensland Water & Land Carers and referenced in partner conservation content.

name: Mounty Aboriginal Youth and Community Service
summary: Mounty Aboriginal Youth and Community Service is an Aboriginal-controlled Mt Druitt youth service supporting community-led programs and justice reinvestment outcomes.
notes: No clear standalone domain was indexed; this appears to be the primary public page and may require Facebook login.

name: Western Sydney Watch Committee
summary: The Watch Committee monitors Aboriginal treatment in custody and advocates on deaths in custody, policing, and justice-system accountability.
notes: Search results tie this to the Aboriginal Deaths in Custody Watch Committee page hosted by Justice Action; specific Western Sydney branding is limited.

name: Our Islands Our Home
summary: Our Islands Our Home is a Torres Strait Islander-led climate justice campaign seeking action to protect Zenadth Kes from climate impacts.
notes: Official campaign website.

name: Mehi Centre
summary: Mehi Centre is a not-for-profit based in Moree that supports Indigenous communities through cultural, educational, environmental, and advocacy initiatives.
notes: Official website includes organisation description and ABN.

name: Martuwarra Fitzroy River Council
summary: Martuwarra Fitzroy River Council is an Indigenous-led alliance of Kimberley Elders advocating to protect the Martuwarra/Fitzroy River system.
notes: Official council website.

name: National Tertiary Education Union
summary: The National Tertiary Education Union is the main union representing university and tertiary education workers across Australia.
notes: Official union website.

name: Fisher's Oysters Pty Ltd
summary: Fishers of Freycinet is a Tasmania-based seafood and oyster business associated with the Fisher family’s farmed oyster operations.
notes: An exact indexed website for the legal entity name 'Fisher's Oysters Pty Ltd' was not clearly found; this is the closest active brand/domain match.

name: First Peoples' Assembly of Victoria
summary: The First Peoples' Assembly of Victoria is an independent, democratically elected body representing Traditional Owners and First Peoples in Victoria in Treaty-related processes.
notes: Official organisation website.

name: Australian Conservation Foundation
summary: The Australian Conservation Foundation is a national environmental advocacy organisation campaigning to protect nature and address climate pollution.
notes: Official organisation website.

name: Limestone Coast Landscape Board
summary: The Limestone Coast Landscape Board is a South Australian statutory board that leads regional landscape and natural resource management.
notes: Official government-hosted board website.

name: Strait Climate Action
summary: Strait Climate Action is described as a Torres Strait youth climate group creating language-based resources and intergenerational climate campaigning.
notes: No standalone official website was discoverable; this URL is a third-party grant page that names and describes the group.

name: North Queensland Land Council
summary: The North Queensland Land Council is the recognised Native Title Representative Body supporting Traditional Owners across its North Queensland region.
notes: Official organisation website.

name: Wardliparingga - SAHMRI
summary: Wardliparingga is SAHMRI’s Aboriginal Health Equity research theme focused on improving health and wellbeing outcomes for Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander peoples.
notes: Wardliparingga appears to be a SAHMRI division rather than a separate legal entity, so the official SAHMRI program page is used.

name: Tribes United
summary: Spiritual Unity of the Tribes is an Australian intertribal community focused on ceremony, cultural continuity, and traditional knowledge sharing.
notes: Could not find a clearly matching standalone organisation explicitly named 'Tribes United'; this is the closest Australian match found.

name: Social Reinvestment WA
summary: Social Reinvestment WA is an Aboriginal-led coalition advocating justice reinvestment and reforms to reduce Aboriginal over-representation in WA’s justice system.
notes: Official organisation website.

name: Human Rights Law Centre
summary: The Human Rights Law Centre is a legal advocacy organisation that works to advance and defend human rights through law reform, litigation, and public campaigning.
notes: Official organisation website.

name: Queensland Teachers' Union
summary: The Queensland Teachers' Union is the industrial and professional union representing educators in Queensland state schools and TAFE.
notes: Official organisation website.

name: Tasmanian Aboriginal Centre
summary: The Tasmanian Aboriginal Centre is an Aboriginal community organisation delivering statewide advocacy, health, cultural, language, and community programs.
notes: Official organisation website.

name: ARDS Aboriginal Corporation
summary: ARDS Aboriginal Corporation is a Yolŋu-led organisation that supports language, culture, education, and community development in Northeast Arnhem Land.
notes: Official organisation website.

name: The Nurse On Foot
summary: The Foot Care Nurse is a nurse-led mobile and clinic foot-care service providing in-home nail and foot health support in Victoria.
notes: No verifiable official web presence for the exact name 'The Nurse On Foot' was found; this is the closest Australian match.

name: Mob Made Media & Communications Agency
summary: Mob Made Media & Communications Agency appears to be a First Nations media and content production agency credited on arts and community campaign work.
notes: No standalone official website was discoverable; this Instagram profile handle is used based on repeated public tagging/credits.

name: First Australians Capital
summary: First Australians Capital is a First Nations-led impact investment and business support organisation backing Indigenous entrepreneurs and enterprises.
notes: Official organisation website.

name: Invasive Species Council
summary: The Invasive Species Council is an Australian environmental advocacy organisation working to prevent and reduce harm from invasive species.
notes: Official organisation website.

name: Pitta Pitta Aboriginal Corporation RNTBC
summary: Pitta Pitta Aboriginal Corporation RNTBC acts as trustee for the native title rights and interests of the Pitta Pitta People.
notes: No standalone corporate website was found; this is the official Native Title prescribed body corporate directory profile.

name: Aboriginal Housing Office
summary: The Aboriginal Housing Office is a NSW statutory body responsible for improving access to affordable housing for Aboriginal people.
notes: Official government agency website.

name: Katji Yanun
summary: Katji Yanun appears to be an Aboriginal-led consulting practice focused on justice, governance, and systems reform advisory work.
notes: No dedicated website or company page was found; this is the principal consultant profile listing Katji Yanun.

name: Justice Reinvestment Port Augusta
summary: Justice Reinvestment Port Augusta appears to be a community-led Port Augusta initiative linked to Aboriginal-led justice reinvestment work delivered via Healthy Dreaming.
notes: No standalone official site for the initiative was found; this URL is the Port Augusta organisation publicly referenced in justice reinvestment program materials.

name: Justice for Walker
summary: Justice for Walker is a grassroots campaign calling for accountability and justice after the death of Warlpiri man Kumanjayi Walker.
notes: No clear standalone official website was found for the campaign itself; this is the most authoritative campaign-specific page located.

name: Pukatja Community
summary: Pukatja Community is an Anangu community on the APY Lands in north-west South Australia.
notes: Used the APY site community page because no distinct standalone website for a separate Pukatja Community Council was found.

name: National Disability Insurance Scheme
summary: The National Disability Insurance Scheme is Australia's national program that funds supports for eligible people with significant and permanent disability.
notes: Official Australian Government NDIS website.

name: Seed Mob
summary: Seed Mob is an Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander youth-led climate justice movement focused on protecting Country and communities.
notes: Official website of Seed Indigenous Youth Climate Network.

name: Pacific Australian Emerging Leaders Network
summary: The Pacific Australian Emerging Leaders Network connects young leaders across the Pacific and Australia for leadership, advocacy, and regional partnership.
notes: Network appears to be coordinated through Micah Australia rather than a separate standalone domain.

name: Murrie Connections
summary: Murrie Connections is an Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander-owned organisation that recruits culturally appropriate participants for research and consulting projects.
notes: Clear standalone official website found.

name: Mapoon Aboriginal Shire Council
summary: Mapoon Aboriginal Shire Council is the local government authority serving the Mapoon community in Cape York, Queensland.
notes: Official council domain.

name: Deadly Inspiring Youth Doing Good ATSI Corporation
summary: Deadly Inspiring Youth Doing Good ATSI Corporation is a youth-led First Nations not-for-profit that empowers young people through leadership and community programs.
notes: Official DIYDG website found with organisation details and contacts.

name: Aboriginal Community Connect
summary: Aboriginal Community Connect is a culturally focused support program helping Aboriginal people address alcohol and other drug issues while staying connected to culture and community.
notes: Appears to be a program under Uniting Communities rather than a separate incorporated entity.

name: Australian Progress
summary: Australian Progress builds the advocacy, leadership, and campaign capacity of social change organisations and civil society movements in Australia.
notes: Clear standalone official website found.

name: Thriving Families SA
summary: Thriving Families SA is a South Australian cross-sector initiative focused on prevention and early supports to improve outcomes for children, families, and communities.
notes: No clear standalone public website surfaced; LinkedIn appears to be the primary official profile.

name: Brisbane Blacks
summary: Brisbane Blacks appears to be a grassroots Aboriginal-led collective associated with community advocacy and political organising in Brisbane.
notes: Results were mostly social media and third-party references, and access to the Facebook page is restricted in search previews.

name: End Toxic Prisons Campaign
summary: End Toxic Prisons Campaign is an abolitionist campaign opposing youth incarceration and advocating community-controlled alternatives, especially for First Nations young people.
notes: Clear campaign website found.

name: First Nations Futures
summary: First Nations Futures is a First Nations-led economic justice organisation that redistributes flexible funding to First Nations community initiatives.
notes: Clear standalone official website found.

name: Woven Project
summary: Woven Project is an Indigenous-led climate justice initiative that builds global Indigenous collaboration, strategy, and advocacy for land rights and climate action.
notes: Search results strongly indicate this is the relevant entity, including references to a Common Threads co-hosted gathering in Australia.

name: Charles Campbell College
summary: Charles Campbell College is a South Australian government Reception-to-Year-12 school in Adelaide.
notes: Official school website found.

name: Digital Storytellers
summary: Digital Storytellers is an Australian social impact storytelling agency that helps purpose-driven organisations create narrative-led campaigns and media.
notes: Clear standalone official website found.

name: Gomeroi Traditional Owners Aboriginal Corporation
summary: This corporation entry represents an Aboriginal organisation connected to Gomeroi/Bigambul traditional owners and family groups.
notes: Could not find a clear standalone website for the exact listed name; closest authoritative result is an ACNC record with a closely related name.

name: Bama Connect Consultant
summary: Bama Connect Consultant provides culturally grounded consulting in Traditional Owner engagement, governance transition support, and on-Country facilitation.
notes: No standalone website surfaced; LinkedIn appears to be the primary official presence.

name: CFN
summary: CFN is a Queensland not-for-profit disability support provider delivering community participation, respite, and daily living services.
notes: CFN is acronym-ambiguous, but this was the strongest Australian organisational match with a complete official site.
